text: Hi, Father. I'm Veda. I'm new to the area and was curious about your church. Could you tell me a little bit about it? | Of course, Veda. Our church is based on the teachings of Jesus Christ. We believe in loving our neighbor and treating others as we would want to be treated. We strive to live according to Christ's example and teachings. | That sounds like a really great way to live. I can see why so many people are drawn to this religion. What do you think makes Christianity different from other religions? | Well, there are a lot of different interpretations of Christianity, but for us, it's all about following Jesus Christ's example. He was a man who loved unconditionally and forgave easily. He preached compassion and understanding, even for those who disagreed with him or did not follow

Hey Shavon, what's up? You seem troubled. | Yeah, I am. I'm just having a hard time and needed someone to talk to. | Of course, man. I'm always here for you. What's going on? | It's just everything. Work is stressing me out, my relationship is falling apart, and I feel like I'm losing touch with my friends. I don't know what to do.

Hi there! Are you looking for someone to take care of your pet while you're out of town? | Yes, I am. I'm going on vacation for a week and I need someone to watch my cat. | That's no problem at all! I love taking care of animals, and I have experience with cats. I'll be happy to take care of your cat while you're gone. | That sounds great. How much do you charge?
Hey, Mattea. What's up? | Not much, what's up with you? | Just hanging out, same old same old. So, what's new with you? | Well, I've been thinking about what you said the other day.

I know what you're thinking, Dontrell. I can sense your feelings and know exactly what you're going to say or do next. You're thinking about how much you miss your family, and how you wish you could see them again. You're wondering if there's anything you can do to make that happen. And you're also feeling a little guilty, because you know that if you could see them again, it would only be for a short while before they died. | Cornell, how can you possibly know all that? It's like you can read my mind! | I told you, I can sense your feelings. It's like I have a connection to your thoughts and emotions. I'm not sure why or how it works, but ever since we met, I've been able to understand what you're thinking and feeling pretty easily. | Wow...that's amazing! And a little bit scary too. But

Hi, God. I'm feeling a little anxious about my math test tomorrow. I'm just not sure if I'm going to be able to do it. Can you give me some guidance? | Of course, Bram. I will always be here for you. Just remember that I am with you and that I will help you through anything. You can do this, Bram. Just have faith and believe in yourself. | Thank you, God. Just knowing that you're with me makes me feel a lot better. I'll try my best and I know that with your help, I can do it. | That's the spirit, Bram! I know you can do it.

You're asking for a lot, Tinsley. We've been partners for a long time and I think you know that we're not in a position to give you what you're asking for. | What exactly are you asking for? | I want a larger percentage of the profits and a say in how the product is marketed. | Why do you think you deserve that?
I'm so glad you're home! I have a surprise for you. | What is it? | Come inside and see! | Okay!

Hi, Pietro. How are you doing today? | I'm doing well, Aariz. Thanks for speaking with me. | Of course! So, what's been going on? | Well, I've just been really busy lately. Work has been keeping me busy and I haven't had much time for anything else.

So, can you tell me a little bit about the job? | Well, it's a customer service position. You'll be working with our clients, helping them with whatever they need. | I see. And what are the qualifications for this job? | Well, we're looking for someone who is friendly and helpful, and who has experience dealing with customers.

I find it amusing that the events of the world are so far removed from my own life. | What do you mean? | Well, take this news story for example. It's about a conflict in some country that I've never even heard of. What does it have to do with me? | I'm not sure. What do you think it has to do with you?

Hey, thanks for taking my motorcycle off my hands. I really appreciate it. | No problem, man. It's a nice bike. I'm sure I'll enjoy riding it. | I'm just glad to be rid of it, to be honest. It was costing me a lot in repairs and maintenance. | Yeah, motorcycles can be pretty expensive to keep up. But they're also a lot of fun to ride. So it's worth it, in my opinion.

"I'm really glad I could help you out, Avarie. I know how much your grandmother means to you." | "Yeah, she's been sick for a while now and I've been meaning to visit her. But with work and everything else, it's been hard to find the time." | "I know how that is. It can be tough to balance everything." | "Yeah. But I'm glad I was finally able to make the trip. And thank you again for driving me to the airport and helping me with everything."
I'm sorry, Daddy. | Why did you let the dog go? | He was whimpering and I felt bad. | Now we'll never find him.

Hi, can I ask you a question? | Of course! What would you like to know? | How long does it usually take for someone to become fluent in a new language? | Well, that really depends on the person. Some people pick up new languages very quickly, while others take a bit longer. There are a lot of factors that can affect how fast someone learns a new language, such as motivation, intelligence, and prior knowledge.

Thank you so much for all your help in selling my house. I really appreciate it. | You're welcome, Deandra. It was my pleasure to help you out. I'm just glad that everything went smoothly and that the house sold quickly. | Yeah, me too. I'm really excited about the move, but it's a bit bittersweet leaving this place behind. It's been my home for so long. | I know how you feel. It can be tough to let go of a place that holds so many memories. But sometimes change can be a good thing, and it sounds like this new opportunity is going to be great for you. Plus, you never know - maybe you'll end up coming back to this city someday and buying another house here! Stranger things have happened. ;)

Sarah, I love the outdoors! I love hiking and camping, and spending time in nature. It's just so beautiful and peaceful out there. | Yeah, I know what you mean. There's just something about being in nature that feels so good for the soul. | I know! I feel so connected to the world when I'm outdoors. It's like everything just makes sense. | Yeah, it's like you can finally breathe and relax.

Hi, I'm Tyrique. | Hi, I'm Lily. Are you flying to London today? | Yes, I am. I'm excited to be visiting the city for the first time. | It's a great city. I'm sure you'll love it. Have you been before?

Hi, Mr. Saunders. I was just wondering if I could ask you a question. | Sure, Curtis. What's on your mind? | I was just wondering... do you have any plans to retire soon? Or sell the bakery? | Oh, I hadn't really thought about it. To be honest, I'm not sure what I would do without this place. It's been my life for so long.
I'm not going back to that school. I hate it. | Why do you keep getting kicked out? | I don't know. I just don't like it there, that's all. And all the kids are phony. | What's a phony?

I'm telling you, man, she is just too good for me. I don't deserve her. | Dude, stop being so negative. You're a great guy and she clearly loves you. | I don't know...I just feel like I'm not good enough for her. She's so amazing and I'm just...I don't know. | Sade, you are an amazing guy and she loves you. Just relax and enjoy it.
I can't believe it's still raining. | I know, dear. It's been raining for days now. | Yeah, I know. I just wish it would stop already. | Well, I don't think it will be stopping anytime soon.

I can't believe I agreed to do this. I'm already so behind on everything else. | What's wrong? You seem really stressed out. | I am. I feel like I can never catch up. Every time I turn around, there's something else that needs to be done. And it's all because I keep taking on too much. | Why do you keep doing that?

Hi, I was just checking my account balance and it's missing $200. Can you tell me what happened? | Let me take a look. When did you last check your balance? | Yesterday. | And how much was in your account then?

Maria, I'm feeling really sick. I have a high fever and a rash all over my body. I'm so weak and tired, all I want to do is just rest. | Oh no, Emmaleigh! You have the measles! You need to go to the doctor right away! | I don't think that's necessary. I'll just drink lots of fluids and rest, and I'll be fine in no time. | Emmaleigh, you could get very sick from the measles. It's best to err on the side of caution and see a doctor just to be sure. Plus, they can give you medication to help make you more comfortable.
Hey True, how are you today? | I'm doing well, Rahim. How about you? | I'm good. So, what have you been up to lately? | Not much. Just hanging out with friends and family. You know, the usual stuff.

I'm really glad you gave me the opportunity to lead the team in your absence. It's something I've been wanting to do for a while now. | Well, you certainly proved yourself today. You did a great job leading the team and handling everything that came up. I'm impressed. | Thank you, I appreciate that. I really wanted to show you that I'm capable of leading and managing the team effectively. | You definitely did that today. You showed excellent leadership skills and made sure everything ran smoothly. I'll be happy to recommend you for future leadership opportunities.

I just feel so trapped, man. I can't even begin to tell you how unhappy I am right now. | Yeah, I can imagine. It's been tough for you lately. | It feels like everything is just falling apart and there's nothing I can do about it. I'm just stuck here, living off my parents and barely scraping by. | Have you looked into getting a job?

Wow, I can't believe I built this all by myself! It looks amazing. | You did a great job, Mahnoor. This building is really impressive. | Thanks, Sarah. I'm pretty proud of it. It took a lot of hard work, but it was worth it. | I can imagine. How long did it take you to build it?

I just feel so stuck in my job. I've been doing the same thing for years and I just can't seem to move up or get out. | Yeah, I can definitely understand how you feel. It's tough when it feels like you're stuck in a rut. But sometimes, it can be helpful to look at your situation from a different perspective. Maybe there are things you could be doing differently in your job that would help you to move up or get out? | I don't know, I've tried everything I can think of. And even if there were things I could be doing differently, I don't know if it would make any difference. It just feels like no matter what I do, I'm not going to get anywhere. | Well, it's worth considering anyway. And even if it doesn't work out the way you want it to, at least you tried something different and made an effo

Hey, Sarah. What's up? | Hey! Not much, what about you? | Same here. Just getting ready for school. I don't really like having to think about what to wear every day, so I just stick to the same outfit. | Yeah, I can understand that. I'm always trying to find new outfits and it's just a lot of work. Plus, it's hard to find something that looks good and is also comfortable.

Hey, Sasha. How are you doing? | I'm doing alright. Just been a little stressed lately. School is just really tough right now. | Yeah, I can imagine. But you're so smart, I'm sure you'll get through it no problem. You always seem to find a way to succeed no matter what the challenge is. | Thanks, Anastazia. That means a lot coming from you. You're just always so confident and everything seems to come so easily to you. I wish I could be more like that.

I can't believe I lost the winning ticket. I'm so angry right now. | I'm sorry to hear that, Mariely. What can I do to help you? | There is nothing you can do. I had the winning ticket and now it's gone. | Well, let's see if we can retrace your steps and see if we can find it. When did you last have it?

Hey, Pedro. What's up? | Not much. Just working on some new invention ideas. What are you up to? | I'm working on a new invention too. I'm trying to create a device that would allow people to communicate with each other without speaking. | That sounds really interesting! I'd love to hear more about it.

I'm almost done. I just have to dry the dishes and put them away. | Okay, sounds good. Let me know when you're finished so I can check everything over. | Will do! | Hey mom, I'm finished with the dishes.

Mom, I know my clothes are muddy, but I had so much fun! Playing in the mud is just so much fun. | Asante, you're going to have to change out of those clothes before you come back inside. You're just going to make a mess if you come inside like that. | | No, Asante, your whole outfit is muddy. You need to take everything off and put on something clean.

I can't believe I've been waiting in this line for so long. It feels like forever. | It's only been five minutes. Relax, it'll be your turn soon. | Yeah, I know. But it feels like forever. | I understand how you feel. Time can seem to move very slowly when we're waiting for something, especially if we're not sure how long it's going to take.

Why are you crying? What's wrong? | I'm just really upset right now. I can't believe you would yell at me over something so small. | I'm sorry, I didn't mean to make you cry. I was just really angry in the moment. But I shouldn't have taken it out on you like that. Can we please talk about this calmly? | I don't know, Casper. I just feel really hurt right now.

Hey, thanks for the ride. | No problem. I'm just glad I could help out. | Yeah, I'm sure your parents would be happy to know you're helping me out. | They're always happy when I help people out. It makes them feel good knowing that I'm a good person.

Hey, Rose. I was thinking about changing my hair back to its original color. | Really? Why? | I don't know. I just feel like people are making fun of me and I don't like it. | Who's making fun of you?
Hi there! How are you today? | I'm doing well, thank you. How are you? | I'm doing great, thanks for asking. So what brings you in today? | I need some new tires for my car.

I can't believe you're the best basketball player in our class. | Yeah, I guess I am. | I've been practicing so hard to be the best, and you just beat me out of nowhere. | Sorry, Gabi. I didn't mean to make you feel bad.

Wow, Laisha. You look absolutely stunning. I can't help but admire your beautiful body. | Thank you, Agatha. I must say, you look pretty good yourself. | I can't help but notice how firm and perky your breasts are. They look absolutely amazing. | Thank you! I've been working hard to keep them looking their best.

Hey, Sarah! I'm so glad you're here. I've been dying to show you my new dance routine. | That sounds great! I can't wait to see it. | Okay, let me just go change into my dancing clothes. I'll be right back! | Sure, no problem.

I just feel like I'm not good enough, you know? No matter what I do, it's never enough. | It sounds like you have some pretty negative thoughts about yourself. Can you tell me more about that? | Yeah. I guess I just feel like I'm always falling short, no matter what. Like, I'll work really hard at something and then it turns out bad or someone else does it better than me and it just feels awful. | That doesn't sound like a very positive way to view yourself or your accomplishments. What do you think might be causing these negative thoughts?
Man, I'm starving. | Yeah, me too. There's nothing to eat around here. | I found a half-eaten sandwich in one of the trashcans. | That's gross! You're not going to eat that, are you?

Hey Alayla, what's up? | Not much, what's up with you? | I'm just feeling really frustrated with how things are going on the soccer team. I feel like I'm not being used to my full potential and it's really frustrating me. | Yeah, I can understand that. It's tough when you feel like you're not being given a chance to show what you can do. But you have to remember that the coach is making the decisions about who plays and who doesn't based on what he thinks is best for the team. Sometimes that means leaving players on the bench who deserve to be playing. It's not personal, it's just business.

I'm really glad to be out of the hospital and back home. It was tough being in there, but I'm feeling a lot better now. | I'm glad to hear that Charla. How are you doing with your medication? Are you still taking it as prescribed? | Yes, I am. And I'm feeling a lot better because of it. My moods are more stable and I'm not having any hallucinations or anything like that anymore. So I guess the medication is working well for me. | That's great to hear! It sounds like you're really making progress with your treatment. But we need to keep an eye on things and make sure that you continue taking your medication as prescribed and attending therapy sessions regularly. If you start to feel like your symptoms are coming back or worsening, please don't hesitate to reach out to us so we can adjust yo
Hey, Sarah. | Hey, Justina. What's up? | Not much. Just hanging out with friends. | Yeah, same here. I'm glad we get to spend some time together. We've been so busy lately.

Hey, coach. Great game today. | You played well out there, Delilah. You were really hustling. | Yeah, I wanted to make sure we got the win. | That's the attitude I like to see. You're a leader out there on the court. Keep it up and you'll go far in this game.

Thank you so much for having me here today. I'm really excited to be part of this exhibit. | We're very grateful to have you here, Andriana. Your help is invaluable in making this exhibit a success. Can you tell me a little bit about your experience with art? | I've been interested in art for as long as I can remember. I used to spend hours drawing and painting when I was younger, and I eventually studied art in college. After graduation, I worked as an artist's assistant for a while before deciding to become a full-time artist myself. So, I've been involved in the art world in one way or another for most of my life. | That's fantastic. We're really lucky to have someone with your expertise on board. Can you tell me a little bit about the piece you're working on for the exhibit?
Hi Mom. | Hi, Sabria. What are you doing? | I'm making a salad for lunch. Do you want some? | That sounds great, thank you. I'll just have a small portion though.

Hi, I'm looking for some new lingerie. Do you have any recommendations? | Of course! We have a lot of great options here. What kind of style are you looking for? | I want something that's sexy but also comfortable. | We have a lot of great choices that fit that description. Let me show you some of our more popular styles.

I remember when you were small. You used to play outside all the time and I loved it. Now I wish I could go back to those days. | Really? Why? | Well, things were just simpler then. We didn't have any responsibilities or worries. We would just run around and play all day long. And it was so much fun watching you grow up and explore the world. | That does sound nice. But don't you think you're being a little nostalgic? Things are different now, but that doesn't mean they're bad.

It's been a long time since I've written anything like this, but I think my book will be able to help people understand what it was like for me as a child soldier. | That's very admirable of you, Claira. Can you tell me a little bit more about the book? What made you want to write it? | Well, I feel like there aren't many stories out there about child soldiers and I wanted to share my own experience. I hope that by reading my story, people will be able to gain a better understanding of what we go through. | I think that's an important perspective to share. How did you come up with the title?

Hi, Officer. I was just in a hit-and-run accident. A car rear-ended me at a stop light and then sped away. I'm feeling really scared and alone right now. | I'm sorry to hear that, Kaelyn. Do you have any idea who might have done this? | No, I don't. It all happened so fast. | Okay. Well, we'll do our best to find the person who did this. In the meantime, try not to worry too much. These things happen sometimes, unfortunately.

Hi, Abrianna. I was wondering if I could ask you for some advice on gardening. | Of course, Ricco. What would you like to know? | Well, I've never done it before and I was wondering where to start. | The first thing you need to do is prepare the soil. You can do this by tilling the ground and adding some compost or manure to help the plants grow. Once the soil is ready, you can plant the seeds. Make sure to water them regularly and give them plenty of sunlight so they can thrive.

That was a great show! I'm so glad we decided to come out tonight. | I know, right? I was a little hesitant at first, but it turned out to be really funny. | Yeah, I'm glad we did it too. It's been a while since we've had a night out like this. | Yeah, I know. We've been so busy lately. It's nice to just relax and laugh for a while.

I'm really proud of you, Augusto. Your hard work is finally paying off. | Thank you, Darrius. It's been a long journey, but I'm finally starting to see some results. | You deserve it. You've worked so hard for this. I can't imagine how difficult it must have been, but you never gave up. | It wasn't easy, that's for sure. But your support meant a lot to me. Knowing that you believed in me even when I didn't believe in myself helped me keep going.

Hey, coach. How're things going? | Hey, Meryl. Things are going good. How about you? | I'm doing great. Just wanted to let you know that I've been training really hard and I'm determined to become a world champion wrestler. | That's great to hear, Meryl. I can tell you've put in the work and you have the talent to make it to the top. Just keep focused and don't let anything get in your way.
Hey, Bella. You doing alright? | Yeah, I'm fine. Just a little tired. | You sure? You look kind of down. | It's just been a long day, that's all.

Hey, thanks for taking the time to talk with me. I really appreciate it. | Of course, no problem. What can I do for you? | Well, like I said, I want to help out with your organisation. I know what it's like to be homeless and I want to help others who are in that situation. | That's great! We always need more volunteers. Are you interested in doing any particular tasks? Cooking, cleaning, interacting with the guests? Let me know and we'll see what we can do.

Hey, boss. What's up? | Not much. Just trying to get some work done. | Yeah, same here. I just wanted to say that I really appreciate everything you've done for me. You've given me a lot of opportunities and I really appreciate it. | Thank you, Chesney. I'm glad you're enjoying your job here.

Hey, Kaitlyn. How's it going? | Good, good. Just getting ready for my day. What about you? | I'm doing well too. I've just been thinking a lot lately about what I want to do with my life and what kind of person I want to be. | What do you mean?

Hi there! Are you lost? I'm happy to help you out. | Oh, thank you! I'm new to the area and I'm looking for the library. | The library is just a few blocks away. Let me walk with you and show you the way. | Thank you, that would be great!
Jazlene, what's the news? | I'm pregnant. | Oh my gosh! That's amazing! When are you due? | October.

I don't understand why our school has a dress code. It's not fair. | What do you mean, it's not fair? What specifically are you upset about? | Well, for one thing, it's sexist. Boys can wear whatever they want but girls have to dress a certain way. It's not right. | I see your point. But the school is just trying to maintain a certain level of decorum and professionalism. They don't want students wearing clothes that are too revealing or inappropriate.

Hi, I'm calling to cancel my cell phone contract. | I'm sorry to hear that. May I ask why you're canceling your contract? | I'm not happy with the service I've been receiving. The coverage is bad and I've been dropped calls. | I'm sorry to hear that. We can certainly try to help you with that. Are you sure you want to cancel your contract?

Doctor, I'm finally feeling better and I really want to be able to move again. I've been bedridden for weeks and I'm extremely weak, but I'm determined to get better. Can you tell me what my prognosis is? | Well, Conner, with time and rehabilitation, you should be able to regain most of your mobility. However, it's important to realistic about your expectations. You may not ever be able to return to your previous level of activity. But if you're willing to put in the work, you can make significant progress. | I'm willing to do whatever it takes. I just want to be able to move again. | Well, then let's get started on your road to recovery.

Father, I want to thank you for giving me the opportunity to lead the congregation in prayer this morning. It is an honor and a privilege to be able to serve you in this way. I pray that my words will be a blessing to those who hear them, and that they will be an encouragement in their faith. Amen. | That was a beautiful prayer, Selin. You did a great job. I'm very proud of you. | Thank you, Father. I just wanted to do my best to honor God. | You certainly did that. I could tell that you were praying with all of your heart. It was very moving.
Hey, Brad. I'm just going to head to my room, okay? | What's your problem? | I don't want to fight with you, so I'm just going to go to my room. | We're not fighting. I just want to talk to you.

Hey, Harry. What's up? | Not much. Just hanging out. What about you? | I'm just trying to get better. You know, perfect myself. | Yeah, I hear you. But don't you think you're being a little too hard on yourself? I mean, you're already pretty good.

Wow, the kitchen looks fantastic! I'm so glad we decided to go ahead and paint it before our party. It really makes a difference. | I know, right? It's amazing what a little bit of paint can do. We should have done this sooner. | Definitely. I'm just glad we were able to get it done in time. Thanks for helping me out with it. | No problem, I was happy to help.

Hi Taylin. It's nice to see you on the train today. | Yeah, I was just thinking the same thing about you. It's been a while since we've seen each other. How have you been? | I've been good. I just moved back to the city recently and I'm still getting used to taking the train every day. | Oh, yeah? I actually just moved here too. I'm still getting used to it as well. But it's been really great so far. I love exploring the city and there's always something new to do.

Hi, boss. Thank you so much for coming to town. I know you're really busy, so I wanted to make sure that everything was taken care of for you. | Thank you, Lupita. You've been a big help. I appreciate everything you've done to make my visit go smoothly. | It's my pleasure. I want to make sure that you have everything you need while you're here. Let me know if there's anything else I can do for you. | Actually, there is one thing...

Hey, Sarah. I'm sorry to bother you, but I was wondering if you could help me find my keys. I can't remember where I left them and I'm starting to get really frustrated. | Of course, Sandi. Where did you last see them? | The last place I remember having them was in my car, but I could have left them anywhere since then. I just don't know where to start looking. | Well, let's start with your car then. Maybe they're just hidden somewhere and you haven't noticed them yet.
I'm thinking about moving to New York City. | Why do you want to move there? | I don't know. I just feel like I need a change. | Have you ever been there before?

Hey, Sarah. I made pasta for dinner tonight and it turned out really well! I was following a recipe to the letter and it paid off. | That's great to hear, Analyse! What kind of pasta dish did you make? | I made a simple spaghetti with tomato sauce. It was really good, and I'm so happy that it turned out well. | That sounds delicious! I'm glad to hear that your cooking experiment was a success.

Hey Jeannie, how's it going? | Hey Jeremey! I'm doing good, just struggling a little bit with this math homework. | Sure, I can help you with that. Let me take a look. | Thanks so much, Jeremey! I really appreciate it.

Hey, Mom. | Hey, baby. How are you feeling? | I'm feeling a little bit excited and nervous. But I know that I am brave and can handle whatever comes my way. | That's great, sweetheart! I'm proud of you for being so positive. School is a big adventure, but you're going to do just fine. You're a smart and strong young man. Just remember to be yourself and have fun!

I'm sorry, Mom. I know I messed up. I was wrong and I want to make things right. | Hannah, I love you no matter what you do but sometimes your choices hurt me. You're not the only one who's hurting here. Your sister is too. | I know, Mom. And I'm sorry. It wasn't just about me. It was about her too and how much she means to me. | What did you do?

Hey, Uncle Joe. | Hey there, Brittaney! How are you doing today? | I'm doing alright. Just trying to stay positive like you always say. | That's the right attitude! You'll get through whatever challenges come your way if you keep that outlook.

You're really upset about this, aren't you? | Yes, I am. I can't believe you would just do something like that without even apologizing. It was really hurtful. | I'm sorry if I hurt you, but I don't feel like apologizing is necessary. I didn't do anything wrong and I don't see why saying sorry would be helpful. | Well, it would show that you're willing to take responsibility for your actions and that you care about my feelings. Otherwise, it just feels like you don't care about me or our relationship.

Hey, Landyn. Are you okay? | No, I'm not okay. Those kids just beat me up for no reason. | Why would they do that? Do you know them? | No, I don't know them. They were just bullying me because they could.

So, I've been pretty busy lately. I've been cooking and cleaning my apartment, running errands around town, and studying for my upcoming exams. It's been a lot, but I'm feeling pretty good about everything. How have you been? | I've been good! A little busy with work, but nothing too crazy. I did want to ask you about your exams though. Are you feeling prepared? | Yeah, I think so. I've been studying a lot and I feel like I have a good handle on the material. I'm just hoping that everything goes well on test day. | I'm sure you'll do great! You're always so prepared for everything. Let me know if you need any help with studying though, I'd be happy to help out.

Juan, I don't know how much longer I can keep this up. I'm always working, and I never have any time for myself. I feel like a machine. | I know you're always busy, mi amor, but you're doing an amazing job. We appreciate everything you do for us. | I appreciate your words, Juan, but they don't change the fact that I'm exhausted. I never have a moment to just relax and recharge. In geography, latitude is a geographic coordinate that specifies the north-south position of a point on the surface of the Earth or another celestial body. Latitude is given as an angle that ranges from −90° at the south pole to 90° at the north pole, with 0° at the Equator. Lines of constant latitude, or parallels, run east-west as circles parallel to the equator.

Longitude is a geographic coordinate that specifies the east-west position of a point on the surface of the Earth, or another celestial body. It is an angular measurement, usually expressed in degrees and denoted by the Greek letter lambda (λ). Meridians are imaginary semicircular lines running from pole to pole that connect points with the same longitude.

A geographic coordinate system (GCS) is a spherical or geodetic coordinate system for measuring and communicating positions directly on Earth as latitude and longitude. It is the simplest, oldest, and most widely used type of the various spatial reference systems that are in use, and forms the basis for most others. Although latitude and longitude form a coordinate tuple like a Cartesian coordinate system, geographic coordinate systems are not Cartesian because the measurements are angles and are not on a planar surface.

In cartography, a map projection is any of a broad set of transformations employed to represent the curved two-dimensional surface of a globe on a plane. In a map projection, coordinates, often expressed as latitude and longitude, of locations from the surface of the globe are transformed to coordinates on a plane. Projection is a necessary step in creating a two-dimensional map and is one of the essential elements of cartography.

In architecture and building engineering, a floor plan is a technical or diagrammatic drawing that illustrates the horizontal relationships of interior spaces or features to one another at one level of a structure. They are typically drawn to-scale and in orthographic projection to represent relationships without distortion. They are usually drawn approximately 4 ft (1.2 m) above the finished floor and indicate the direction of north.
The four cardinal directions or cardinal points are the four main compass directions: north (N), east (E), south (S), and west (W). The corresponding azimuths are 0°, 90°, 180°, and 270°.

In navigation, dead reckoning is the process of calculating the current position of a moving object by using a previously determined position, or fix, and incorporating estimates of speed, heading, and elapsed time. The corresponding term in biology, to describe the processes by which animals update their estimates of position or heading, is path integration.

A continental divide is a drainage divide on a continent such that the drainage basin on one side of the divide feeds into one ocean or sea, and the basin on the other side either feeds into a different ocean or sea, or else is endorheic, not connected to the open sea. Every continent on Earth except Antarctica has at least one continental drainage divide; islands, even small ones like Killiniq Island on the Labrador Sea in Canada, may also host part of a continental divide or have their own island-spanning divide. The endpoints of a continental divide may be coastlines of gulfs, seas or oceans, the boundary of an endorheic basin, or another continental divide.

A river delta is a landform, typically triangular, created by the deposition of the sediments that are carried by the waters of a river, where the river merges with a body of slow-moving water or with a body of stagnant water. The creation of a river delta occurs at the river mouth, where the river merges into an ocean, a sea, or an estuary, into a lake, a reservoir, or into another river that cannot carry away the sediment supplied by the feeding river. Etymologically, the term river delta derives from the triangular shape (Δ) of the uppercase Greek letter delta.

A floodplain or flood plain or bottomlands is an area of land adjacent to a river. Floodplains stretch from the banks of a river channel to the base of the enclosing valley, and experience flooding during periods of high discharge. The soils usually consist of clays, silts, sands, and gravels deposited during floods.

A valley is an elongated low area often running between hills or mountains and typically containing a river or stream running from one end to the other. Most valleys are formed by erosion of the land surface by rivers or streams over a very long period. Some valleys are formed through erosion by glacial ice.

A canyon, gorge or chasm, is a deep cleft between escarpments or cliffs resulting from weathering and the erosive activity of a river over geologic time scales. Rivers have a natural tendency to cut through underlying surfaces, eventually wearing away rock layers as sediments are removed downstream. A river bed will gradually reach a baseline elevation, which is the same elevation as the body of water into which the river drains.

An estuary is a partially enclosed coastal body of brackish water where freshwater from rivers or streams meets and mixes with saltwater from the open sea. Estuaries form transition zones between riverine and marine environments and are classified as ecotones, areas where different ecosystems overlap. They are influenced by both marine processes and fluvial processes.

In geology and physical geography, a plateau, also called a high plain or a tableland, is an area of highland consisting of flat terrain that is raised sharply above the surrounding area on at least one side. Often one or more sides have deep escarpments or hills. Plateaus can be formed by a number of processes, including upwelling of volcanic magma, extrusion of lava, and erosion by water and glaciers.

In geomorphology, a butte is an isolated hill with steep, often vertical sides and a small, relatively flat top; buttes are smaller landforms than mesas, plateaus, and tablelands. The word butte comes from the French word butte, meaning 'knoll' ; its use is prevalent in the Western United States, including the Southwest, where mesa is used for the larger landform.

A mesa is an isolated, flat-topped elevation, ridge, or hill, bounded from all sides by steep escarpments and standing distinctly above a surrounding plain. Mesas consist of flat-lying soft sedimentary rocks, such as shales, capped by a resistant layer of harder rock, like sandstone or limestone, forming a caprock that protects the flat summit. The caprock may also include dissected lava flows or eroded duricrust.
